reasoning: The resolution criteria explicitly require a formal declaration of war, explicitly excluding border skirmishes, surgical strikes, and proxy wars. A major military crisis occurred between India and Pakistan in May 2025—frequently referred to as the 'India-Pakistan Crisis of 2025' or the 'Four Days in May'. During this substantial escalation (the most intense since 1971), there was no formal declaration of war. In fact, media explainers from early May 2025 noted that India has never formally declared war even during past major wars with Pakistan, and lacks a specific constitutional mechanism to do so. With a US-mediated ceasefire declared on May 10, 2025, the situation has de-escalated. Given that the highest level of conflict in decades did not trigger a formal declaration of war, the chance of one happening before December 31, 2025, is practically zero.
